signatures.label: minute hour day_of_month month day_of_week command

documentation.value:
Defines a cron job schedule. Each field specifies when the command runs.
```crontab
# Every day at 2:30 AM
30 2 * * * /usr/bin/backup.sh

# Every 5 minutes
*/5 * * * * /usr/bin/check_health.sh

# Weekdays at 9 AM
0 9 * * 1-5 /usr/bin/report.sh
```
Minute (0-59). Supports `*`, ranges (`1-5`), lists (`1,15,30`), and steps (`*/5`).
Hour (0-23). Supports `*`, ranges, lists, and steps.
Day of month (1-31). Supports `*`, ranges, lists, and steps.
Month (1-12 or JAN-DEC). Supports `*`, ranges, lists, and steps.
Day of week (0-7, where 0 and 7 are Sunday, or SUN-SAT). Supports `*`, ranges, lists, and steps.
The shell command to execute.

Runs a command once at system startup.
```crontab
@reboot /usr/bin/start_server.sh
```
The shell command to execute at boot.

Runs a command once every hour (equivalent to `0 * * * *`).
```crontab
@hourly /usr/bin/sync.sh
```
The shell command to execute every hour.

Runs a command once every day at midnight (equivalent to `0 0 * * *`).
```crontab
@daily /usr/bin/cleanup.sh
```
The shell command to execute daily.

Runs a command once a week on Sunday at midnight (equivalent to `0 0 * * 0`).
```crontab
@weekly /usr/bin/weekly_report.sh
```
The shell command to execute weekly.

Runs a command once a month on the 1st at midnight (equivalent to `0 0 1 * *`).
```crontab
@monthly /usr/bin/monthly_backup.sh
```
The shell command to execute monthly.

Runs a command once a year on January 1st at midnight (equivalent to `0 0 1 1 *`).
```crontab
@annually /usr/bin/yearly_archive.sh
```
The shell command to execute annually.

Sets an environment variable for all subsequent cron jobs in the crontab.
```crontab
SHELL=/bin/bash
PATH=/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin
MAILTO=admin@example.com
```
Environment variable name (e.g. `SHELL`, `PATH`, `MAILTO`, `HOME`).
Value to assign to the variable.
